title: Unlisted.dat
description: A subtle yet deeply unnerving psychological horror experience embedded seamlessly into Minecraft. You’ll start to question who—or what—is really playing.
body:
Unlisted.dat is a minimalist psychological horror mod that transforms the familiar comfort of singleplayer Minecraft into something far more uncertain. There are no clear instructions, no overt enemies, and no obvious threats — just an ever-present feeling that something isn’t quite right.

The mod integrates directly into vanilla survival gameplay, gradually building tension over time. The horror is designed to be subtle, unpredictable, and persistent — creating an experience that stays with you long after you log out.

Perfect for players who enjoy analog horror, psychological tension, or the creeping fear of being watched.


<details>
<summary>Mod's Content (Spoilers)</summary>

- Adds an entity, that rarely spawns during night, will keep looking at you, until you approach it very close... If you go to close, will either become a hallucination, or come for your blood. (Becomes hostile)

</details>



## 🧠 Key Features

🔸 Vanilla-friendly

🔸 Designed for long-term survival play

🔸 Works in both singleplayer and multiplayer

🔸 Audio and visual cues optimized for headphones and immersive environments

## ⚠️ Warnings

This mod includes psychological horror and ambient tension

May not be suitable for players sensitive to fear or isolation

Best experienced alone, with headphones, at night

Recommended to play with Insanity Shader, for the best in-game horror experience.

Please also read FAQ, before downloading the mod or askig any questions.

## ❓ FAQ

Q: Does this mod crash my game/PC or grief my world?
A: No. It does not crash your device, and never will, it does not grief your world too, but maybe in the future versions, the monster would be able to break any block in its way.

Q: Does the mod have jumpscares?
A: Currently, as of the demo version, no.

Q: What programs were used to make this mod?
A: For modeling, Blockbench, and for everything else, MCreator.
